Events

Pre-1600
 240 – Shapur I becomes co-emperor of the Sasanian Empire with his father Ardashir I.
 467 – Anthemius is elevated to Emperor of the Western Roman Empire.
 627 – King Edwin of Northumbria is converted to Christianity by Paulinus, bishop of York.
1012 – Duke Oldřich of Bohemia deposes and blinds his brother Jaromír, who flees to Poland.
1204 – The Crusaders of the Fourth Crusade breach the walls of Constantinople and enter the city, which they completely occupy the following day.

1601–1900
1606 – The Union Flag is adopted as the flag of English and Scottish ships.
1776 – American Revolution: With the Halifax Resolves, the North Carolina Provincial Congress authorizes its Congressional delegation to vote for independence

(A) [step] If you are planting your carnations in a field, sow 4 inches (10 cm) apart. [title] Cut away ¼ inch (0.6 cm) of soil at about an angle.  (B) [step] You should sow at least 2 inches (5.1 cm) apart. Fresh carnations don't need to be planted immediately before october 1st.  (C) [step] Cover with 1/4 inch (. 6 cm) soil [title] Mist the seeds occasionally to promote moisture.  (D) [step] Aim for a growing pot with plenty of space for germination. [substeps] Choose a pot that is about 6 inches (15 cm) deep.

[header] How to do a handover in an office [title] Discuss the handover with your manager. [step] You should start by having a detailed discussion with your manager to determine exactly how they want you to handle the handover. Depending on your job and how your manager likes to work, you may be asked to contribute extensively to the handover.
(A) You may end up helping the director post handover team members off at a later date as they lift the promotion. [substeps] Discussing the handover with your manager in person could look particularly good in their office, but you may want to schedule some time to discuss with them before the meeting.  (B) [substeps] Your manager may be able to convince you to participate in the handover. Or, they may need to have you write a letter that includes details about the handover or plan to participate.  (C) Go on to discuss what your boss would like you to do on handover. [substeps] If your manager is unsure about your willingness to contribute on handover, provide specific examples of your eagerness to contribute and explain exactly why you do not feel you are contributing to the handover.  (D) This could include the new person shadowing you for a few days or longer. [substeps] In most instances you will have to at least prepare a formal handover document.

[header] How to check the imei number of an iphone [title] Open your iphone's settings. [step] From the springboard screen of your iphone, tap the app with a gear icon labeled " settings " to view your iphone's customizable options. [title] View your device's information.
(A) [step] Tap the settings icon listed in the sources section of the settings page. Depending on your device, a list of your current contacts will be displayed along with the device name, model, or phone number, along with apple id and other info.  (B) [step] There are four options on the springboard : [substeps] Cellular cellular-you'll need to enter the phone number to search. You can find this information by tapping the " cellular " option.  (C) [step] Tap the " public " tab, which will bring you to a list of all your main devices and their imei numbers. Tap the " imei numeric " button on the left to view your device's imei number.  (D) [step] Scroll down the settings and tap the " general " options. [title] Select " about " from the general settings list, and you should see all the information about your iphone such as its software version, serial number, and more.
answer: (D) [step] Scroll down the settings and tap the " general " options. [title] Select " about " from the general settings list, and you should see all the information about your iphone such as its software version, serial number, and more.

[header] How to remove scuff marks from car vinyl [title] Create a homemade vinegar-based cleaner. [step] If you don't want to purchase a specially made degreaser or scuff remover, you can try making a homemade cleaner first. Combine equal parts of white vinegar and water.
(A) Add a few drops of the vinegar and water and mix well. This mixture is well-acidic and is suitable for dealing with metal or hard surfaces, like rubber.  (B) Pour the mixture into a clean spray bottle. [title] Use a magic eraser to buff out scuffs.  (C) After you've combined the two liquids, combine them with a fine-headed spoon to form a solution. [substeps] The solution should be about 60% vinegar and 60% water.  (D) Incorporate 5 parts vinegar to 7 parts water, then mix them together. Allow the cleaner to sit in the vinegar as it dissolves the scuff marks.
answer: (B) Pour the mixture into a clean spray bottle. [title] Use a magic eraser to buff out scuffs.

(A) You may also consider taking a blood test to see if there are any normalities that are preventing your symptoms. In this case, your doctor might suggest that you visit a physical therapist who will help you address your symptoms.  (B) He or she will discuss treatment options and suggest preparing an effective plan for you. Typically, your doctor will prescribe : [substeps] Blood tests.  (C) Some symptoms, while rare, will be related to different types of heart conditions. [substeps] For example, people with blood-thinning heart disease may experience none of the following symptoms: pain or increased blood pressure, feeling like the condition is urgent, or feeling like you have a heavy heartbeat.  (D) However, some common symptoms that have occurred because of heart cancer are listed in the following steps. [substeps] Keep in mind that the following symptoms could also be symptoms of different medical conditions, so if you experience several of these symptoms, contact your doctor and set up some tests (these tests will be covered in the next section).

question:
[header] How to make your bedroom asthma friendly [title] Vacuum your bedroom once a week. [step] Use a vacuum that employs a hepa filter or another small-pore, multi-layered vacuum bag designed to help trap allergens. Vacuums with motorized heads are also recommended, as they are better at capturing the dust that the vacuum agitates.
(A) You will need to use only one vacuum bag per room at a time, though. [title] Treat your walls with organic odors.  (B) Hepa filters can be purchased in kits ranging from a few sizes to additional sizes depending on your needs. [substeps] Hepa filters use a pressure pressure cap that allows an individual to control the amount of dust trapped in the vacuum.  (C) Be sure to get into every nook and cranny in your room each time you vacuum. [substeps] Vacuum any carpeted areas in your home twice a week, making sure to slowly vacuum the entire surface.  (D) [substeps] Don't forget to vacuum the carpets and under your bed, too. A near-daily cleaning is worth a good start.
answer: (C) Be sure to get into every nook and cranny in your room each time you vacuum. [substeps] Vacuum any carpeted areas in your home twice a week, making sure to slowly vacuum the entire surface.

[header] How to change gmail address [title] Sign out of your current account. [step] If you're currently logged into your gmail account, you'll need sign out before proceeding further. [substeps] From your inbox, click on your user icon in the upper right corner of the page.
(A) Clicking lets you go back to your current account. If you've changed your username and password on your old account, you'll see them there.  (B) Click on the email box containing the email address you want to change your account from. Click the link for this email address on the menu next to " completely change account.  (C) Click the gear-down button to the right of the search box. All of your gmail addresses are listed here.  (D) Click on the " sign out " button from the resulting pop-up menu to log out of your account. Once you sign out, you should be automatically redirected to the gmail homepage.
answer: (D) Click on the " sign out " button from the resulting pop-up menu to log out of your account. Once you sign out, you should be automatically redirected to the gmail homepage.

question:
[header] How to utilize winter break for studying [title] Take a couple days off. [step] When your break begins, you may feel burned out from school and excited about having time off. Take a couple days off to clear your mind recharge so you can return to your studies with motivation.
(A) [substeps] Let yourself do something fun as a reward to offset any holiday class you may have missed. Listen to music, do a relaxing activity on a break, or join a fun class.  (B) [substeps] Find a company that allows you to supplement your time. Look on the company's website or call or email to make sure you have permission to take breaks or get lunch in between your breaks.  (C) Go on and sleep in, play video games, and do whatever else you want. Refreshing your mind will help you study better later.  (D) [substeps] There are several busy holidays coming up. If you are homesick and want to return to school, take a couple days off.
answer: (C) Go on and sleep in, play video games, and do whatever else you want. Refreshing your mind will help you study better later.

question:
[header] How to organize a small office or guest room [title] Measure from floor to ceiling and back again! [step] Don't buy a desk, file cabinet, bookcase or bed without comparing sizes. A big mistake many folks make is buying something they like, bringing it home, and then finding out the object is taking up too much of the room. [title] Go up, not out.
(A) [step] This is critical to utilizing your space. A desk just deep enough for the computer with lots of shelves going up towards the ceiling to hold cd's, books, etc.  (B) [step] Choose a desk, filing cabinet, filing cupboard, bookshelf, etc. Where you can go to accumulate all of your materials, and hold it up without looking.  (C) [step] What you don't need is tons of different things lying around. Get clothes on hangers and hang them on up to achieve this look.  (D) [step] If the wall is sticking out of your way, then eliminate the wall it to the wall and move out. That will only make your space feel more overwhelmed and unappealing.
answer: (A) [step] This is critical to utilizing your space. A desk just deep enough for the computer with lots of shelves going up towards the ceiling to hold cd's, books, etc.

question:
[header] How to treat avian pox in button quail [title] Know the symptoms of the pox. [step] It is a viral disease that comes in both wet and dry forms. Birds of all ages can be infected, but quail chicks are particularly vulnerable.
(A) Once the infection begins it can take between three and five weeks for the virus to finish its course. [substeps] When infected with the wet form of the virus, a quail will suffer from irritation in the respiratory system, including internal lesions.  (B) [substeps] Since the pox is contagious within a few hours of the first killing of the chickens, the disease may infect the sheds and/or other structures in the flock or surrounding buildings. It is particularly easy to diagnose and treat the pox in dry weather, so be sure you check that gardens and coop equipment are accessible.  (C) [substeps] It is usually not part of the breeding season for quail. However, if you are sick with the pox, you should bring along a bulb or a small syringe for disinfectant.  (D) You should watch out for signs that the infection comes into contact with your quail's hind quarters and other areas. [substeps] The parasites that cause the pox are also at risk.
answer: (A) Once the infection begins it can take between three and five weeks for the virus to finish its course. [substeps] When infected with the wet form of the virus, a quail will suffer from irritation in the respiratory system, including internal lesions.

It is particularly easy to diagnose and treat the pox in dry weather, so be sure you check that gardens and coop equipment are accessible.  (C) [substeps] It is usually not part of the breeding season for quail. However, if you are sick with the pox, you should bring along a bulb or a small syringe for disinfectant.  (D) You should watch out for signs that the infection comes into contact with your quail's hind quarters and other areas. [substeps] The parasites that cause the pox are also at risk.
Answer:

[header] How to use photography in modern advertising [title] Decide your message. [step] In the case of most advertisements, this is the 1 thing that you want to sell. Avoid the trap of choosing more than 1 thing, leading to a cluttered, unclear advertisement.
(A) [title] Brainstorm possible photographs that could convey your message. [step] This can be anything from product shots to people using the product to landscapes or situations that show the kind of person who buys your product.  (B) Look at how much work you would do without proper photography equipment when designing your advertisement. If the advertisement is based on a piece of art, your audience will be more likely to buy.  (C) Instead, look for the easiest, most simple and efficient way to sell your screenshots. [title] Locate a suitable location to show your shots.  (D) [title] Focus on defining what you want your advertisement to have. [step] An advertisement for a clothing business, for example, could feature an ad titled ,' shapely women in schools' looking to solve many of their own problems.

question:
[header] How to keep your dog comfortable indoors [title] Provide a comfortable dog bed. [step] Keep your dog cozy indoors with a great dog bed suited for its particular needs. If your dog is shorthaired and often shivers, get it a cave-like bed with a fluffy overhang.
(A) You can also add these beds to your house as well, depending on your dog's specific needs. [substeps] If your dog enjoys having its beds cozy, place them in areas of your home that are less likely to be exposed to the elements.  (B) If your longhaired dog is prone to getting hot, use a cot-style bed without any fluff. [substeps] Consider getting an orthopedic memory foam bed for your older dog with joint problems.  (C) Whichever bed you choose, make sure it is big enough. You can even try using a beanbag or other bed that you can climb on into.  (D) [substeps] A cave is an air-conditioned enclosure located out of the elements which is filled with loose blankets and straw. [title] Keep your dog's caretaker asleep inside.
answer: (B) If your longhaired dog is prone to getting hot, use a cot-style bed without any fluff. [substeps] Consider getting an orthopedic memory foam bed for your older dog with joint problems.

question:
[header] How to use a sextant [title] Know, if possible, your position above sea level. [step] If you're not using the sextant from aboard a ship at sea, you'll have to correct your sighting for your height above sea level. We'll discuss the correction later; for now, you just need to know your elevation above sea level.
(A) [title] Sight the horizon by looking through the horizon mirror. [step] The horizon mirror is only partially silvered, allowing you to look through it and through the sighting scope beyond it.  (B) [title] Move your sextant to higher magnification than your actual height for the easiest and safest landing. [step] The additional magnification will provide you with more natural visibility.  (C) [title] Know, however, that it will take longer to change sexes or sizes. [step] How long it takes you to change sexes from a fly-up to a mosquito is up to you, but it will help with the work.  (D) [title] If landing on an open sea, raise your cv/bmp to 1000 mpg to face up. [step] To gain height following landing, don't lift up your cv/bmp, but lift up your fps or bmp.
answer: (A) [title] Sight the horizon by looking through the horizon mirror. [step] The horizon mirror is only partially silvered, allowing you to look through it and through the sighting scope beyond it.

question:
[header] How to stop being controlling [title] Stop being a perfectionist. [step] One of the reasons you may be so controlling is because you have the desire for everything to be perfect. You may not want people over if your place isn't spotless; you may spend an extra hour scanning a report for typos without finding any.
(A) If you find that everything you do is absolutely perfect in a given situation, stop doing it. You need to establish a goal for yourself.  (B) These unrealistic instincts are not due to your irrational self-talk: you want perfection, and you would not judge others based on their list of flaws. You need to develop a work ethic that works to get what you want.  (C) After a while, this kind of behavior doesn't help you or anybody else. In fact, it is only hurting you and holding you back from living your life.  (D) Stop being a perfectionist, and try to play things down until you are in control again. [substeps] Perfectionism is one of the most common behavior issues associated with controlling people.
answer: (C) After a while, this kind of behavior doesn't help you or anybody else. In fact, it is only hurting you and holding you back from living your life.

question:
[header] How to pay for the emergency room [title] Know where you're covered. [step] Choosing the right hospital can make all the difference. Do your research before you need to go to the emergency room so you will know exactly where to go when an emergency happens.
(A) [substeps] Check out the websites like emergency services, emergency services, and hospitals as well as the sites like mantras or local resources. You can also research health services in your area as well.  (B) For example, you may find emergency rooms outside of a hospital and there may be emergency rooms close by, ready for anyone requiring emergency medical attention. Do your research ahead of time by looking on the internet and taking a look around nearby facilities.  (C) [substeps] If you have insurance, make sure you know which hospitals are considered in-network and which are considered out-of-network. Your bill will likely be much lower if you choose an in-network facility.  (D) If someone in your family is covered for emergency care when you're not, you can actually get sick while you're out. Private hospitals typically want emergency rooms to be checked first, but only if they have clear medical protocols in place.
answer: (C) [substeps] If you have insurance, make sure you know which hospitals are considered in-network and which are considered out-of-network. Your bill will likely be much lower if you choose an in-network facility.

[header] How to look beautiful [title] Find the right shampoo and conditioner. [step] Certain products work better with certain hair types. Ask a professional or take the time to experiment.
(A) [substeps] Shampoo the roots and condition the ends. This prevents build-up on your scalp and keeps the ends from breaking.  (B) Shampoo and conditioner should not be used before you have your hair dry, but that will depend on your hair type. Each type of shampoo has its own instructions, so be very honest in your selection and get one that's right for your hair.  (C) It's better to keep a hair care guide with you so you can be sure you're getting the right brands, moisturizers, and products. Always remember to shampoo and condition your hair in the shower.  (D) [substeps] There are tons of different shampoos, conditioners, and facial scrubs available. The solution is to buy the one closest to your hair type.
answer: (A) [substeps] Shampoo the roots and condition the ends. This prevents build-up on your scalp and keeps the ends from breaking.

question:
[header] How to tackle [title] Maintain the proper position and tackling form. [step] Your head should be up and your eyes should be on the ball carrier at all times when you're playing defense. Your feet should be shoulder-width apart, with your knees bent and your shoulders back.
(A) When blocking, your arms should be straight and your arms don't swing or slide. While offensive players can perfect how to tackle from far back, some players who prioritize an offensive position tend to be better at tackling from front or side.  (B) Ideally, you want to have your back at about 45 degrees relative the ground. [substeps] This is sometimes referred to as " breakdown " position.  (C) Your back should be parallel to the ball. Your shoulders should be near your hips, rather than touching.  (D) Place the ball carrier clear of your field of vision or your opponents view. Minimize incoming chance of injury and generate power in your technique.
answer: (B) Ideally, you want to have your back at about 45 degrees relative the ground. [substeps] This is sometimes referred to as " breakdown " position.

question:
[header] How to teach swim lessons for kids [title] Remember that safety comes first. [step] Before teaching a new skill, one must make sure that everyone involved stays safe. Never turn your back on a beginner.
(A) Be cognizant of the dangers involved in swimming, which include drowning, equipment malfunctions, or slipping. Be up-to-date in your understanding of cpr and first aid.  (B) Be upfront and tell everyone to stay on the board as you teach them the water. Ask each child when they want to switch out and where they want to change their board there, but don't encourage them to switch over while swimming.  (C) You might see a biker taking a trip one day with a wet ponytail hanging down behind her back or you might see someone climbing out the front of the car and holding a bow and arrow. These are ways to ensure that safety is in doubt.  (D) Watch the child and make sure he can lead the boat. It's a good idea to teach how to operate from the back of the boat, since if he can't, he or she could drown.
answer: (A) Be cognizant of the dangers involved in swimming, which include drowning, equipment malfunctions, or slipping. Be up-to-date in your understanding of cpr and first aid.

question:
[header] How to prevent and manage sciatic pain [title] Stand up straight with your ears aligned with your shoulders. [step] Sitting puts extra stress on your lower back. Sit with your knees slightly elevated to reduce pressure on your spine.
(A) [substeps] Your body will need to relax during a sciatic nerve fracture. Sitting will stretch out your spine and thus will make it easier to sustain a sciatic nerve fracture.  (B) Keep your arms straight and your palms on your thighs. [substeps] Avoid opening up your head, which will lead to more painful straining.  (C) Make sure your back and buttocks sit against the back of the chair. It is a good idea to keep the spine moving throughout the day to prevent stiffness in the vertebrates involved with sciatica (l4-s4).  (D) Using an arm or leg can increase blood flow to your lower back and prevent it from getting worse. [title] Sit in a standing position for at least 20 seconds per day.
answer: (C) Make sure your back and buttocks sit against the back of the chair. It is a good idea to keep the spine moving throughout the day to prevent stiffness in the vertebrates involved with sciatica (l4-s4).

(D) Using an arm or leg can increase blood flow to your lower back and prevent it from getting worse. [title] Sit in a standing position for at least 20 seconds per day.

question:
[header] How to use accent lighting in a stairwell [title] Install wall sconces along the stairwell. [step] A classic and simple way to use accent lighting in a staircase is to install a few wall sconces. Sconces can be placed at regular intervals along the wall, with their heights graduated to follow the slope of the stairs.
(A) When installing wall sconces, you'll want to have at least 24 lamps placed on each tread. However, hang a few lamps on opposite sides of the stairs to pick more subdued tones if you have a bathroom and want a warm, peaceful feel to a familiar staircase.  (B) This type of lighting scheme will cast a gentle wash of light upwards onto your wall, which often helps to make a cramped staircase feel more open. [title] Wire some spotlights to highlight nearby artwork.  (C) Ambient lighting is ideal, but also easier to install in an indoor setting. [substeps] A sleek, flat surface like slate will work well, as will sconces that alternate between bold and contrasting colors.  (D) [title] Space out the sconces so that they are spaced at different angles. [step] Keep in mind that wall sconces will have differently sloped edges, so in some cases you may need to strategically arrange the fixtures so that they are further apart.
answer: (B) This type of lighting scheme will cast a gentle wash of light upwards onto your wall, which often helps to make a cramped staircase feel more open. [title] Wire some spotlights to highlight nearby artwork.

question:
[header] How to teach teens about the dangers of credit cards [title] Do your research. [step] Read up on credit card companies, the national debt average, and interest rates. Did you know credit card companies are allowed to visit many high schools and colleges? You may want to warn your teen.
(A) They will probably ask questions, so be prepared to answer them. [title] Talk to them sooner, rather than later.  (B) [title] Leave out the high-interest card offers and ask what their programs will offer. [step] You may use a law or etiquette guide to make sure your teen understands the interest rates on credit cards, but also wish to understand the science behind the card.  (C) If they don't answer your questions, be in charge and tell them. Of course, you should also tell your teen about the low interest rates on a credit card.  (D) Some of these high-interest places are likely to offer credit card cards : [substeps] Airports restaurants public schools colleges [title] Discuss several ways to treat credit card debt. [step] Write down the specific behaviors or tendencies you want to show your teen about the credit card's repercussions.
answer: (A) They will probably ask questions, so be prepared to answer them. [title] Talk to them sooner, rather than later.

question:
[header] How to sue for contract fraud [title] Ask for recommendations. [step] As soon as you suspect that fraud may have played a role in the execution of a contract between you and another party, you should contact a lawyer for advice. Even if you do not end up hiring a lawyer, they may be able to tell you whether fraud may have been at play.
(A) Start your lawyer search by asking friends and family for recommendations. Lawyers are usually people involved with the community and because of that many people often know one.  (B) Most likely, an attorney will know what you need help with, and can help outline the route you should take to seek employment. [substeps] You should get a referral to a lawyer who specializes in contracts.  (C) Call legal aid organizations to ask for referrals. [substeps] If you decide to involve someone other than the other party on the lawsuit, your lawyer should be able to point you in the direction of a trained and knowledgeable attorney.  (D) [substeps] Your lawyer can handle the entire case and give you some tips on how to file a lawsuit. You may be able to consult an attorney yourself, but ask friends or coworkers who've sued you for recommendations who have a good understanding of the law.
answer: (A) Start your lawyer search by asking friends and family for recommendations. Lawyers are usually people involved with the community and because of that many people often know one.

[step] The element calcium follows the trend in properties that is seen with magnesium, sodium and potassium elements. Namely, it is toxic to mammals in its metallic form, while it is benign in its ionic form.
(A) In order to really understand the balance between these two substances, study periodic urine division to find out the balance between two components of magnesium ions. [title] Study periodic formula formulas to complete the formula for magnesium ions.  (B) The characteristic start and end voltage adjacent to the nodes of platinum and magnesium ions is the reverse. In order to absorb magnesium ions, plasma cells are needed to be able to filter them and charge them. [step] These minerals and fatty acids are easier to digest than fatty acids.  (D) Actually, calcium ions are not only benign but also essential for the metabolism of the body. [title] Study the use of calcium by the body.

question:
[header] How to make pork gravy [title] Know that pork gravy is made from the drippings of cooked pork. [step] In order to collect the drippings to make the gravy, you need to cook the pork first. This can be done in one of two ways.
(A) Either you cook the pork in direct boiling water (the choice is up to you. The indirect cooking method is commonly performed as well.  (B) First, you will get the drippings into a bowl, which will immediately get into the gravy. That means that because you intend to cook your pork, you need to partially cook the pork first.  (C) [substeps] Roast the pork: select your cut of pork and place it in a oven-proof dish or pan fat-side down. Preheat the oven to 400 ° f (204 ° c).  (D) You can cook the pork into gravy or simmer it, which provides a richer gravy, and put it in an oil greased skillet to broil. The drippings in a gravy can also be infused into the gravy : [substeps] Raw beef broil ground beef hot.
answer: (C) [substeps] Roast the pork: select your cut of pork and place it in a oven-proof dish or pan fat-side down. Preheat the oven to 400 ° f (204 ° c).

[header] How to turn off the repetitively delayed push notifications from incoming messages on an ios device's message app [title] Locate and open the preinstalled settings app on the ios device that is displaying the repetitively delayed message push notification. [step] This will give you a list of settings you can choose from to set up your notifications for the device. [title] Tap notifications from the list of the main page of settings in the settings app.
(A) [step] Tap these settings and tap uninstall incoming incoming messages from the inbox. This will apply the setting for incoming messages, location registries and notifications.  (B) [title] Swipe down and tap voiceover. [step] The process will take approximately 15 seconds.  (C) [title] Look for and tap the " messages " item. [title] Scroll down until you reach the very bottom of the settings.  (D) [title] Tap unfollow from the notification panel. [step] This will restrict the option to be set to " off " from activating again.

question:
[header] How to get along with your sister [title] Support your sister's achievements. [step] If you want a good relationship with your sister, make her feel supported. Instead of becoming jealous over your sister's achievements, be her personal cheerleader.
(A) Show her how great she is by always trying your best. [title] Know when it's time to talk.  (B) Help her celebrate the new trends in her life by telling her about what you think of her when you see her test the cool advanced week of her next year. Be genuine in your remarks and compliments, and your sister will be impressed by them.  (C) When your sister achieves something, avoid judging her as she might criticize you. Let her see that no one compares to her.  (D) This will make your sister feel valued and strengthen your bond. [substeps] If your sister achieves something, offer her a sincere congratulations.
answer: (D) This will make your sister feel valued and strengthen your bond. [substeps] If your sister achieves something, offer her a sincere congratulations.

question:
[header] How to get your boyfriend to hold hands with you again [title] Break the touch barrier. [step] When you are with your boyfriend, try to touch him more than you normally do. This will help make him more comfortable with touching you and he will feel more confident about holding your hand if he is nervous.
(A) You can start with a light touch and change the touch as each touch gets intimate. For example, try just holding his hand when you go out for coffee or dinner. question:
[header] How to stretch your knee [title] Use dynamic stretches to strengthen and stretch your muscles. [step] This exercise works your thighs and supports good body mechanics-including how your knee moves-if it's done properly. Start with a smaller range or motion and keep your movements within your pain-free zone.
(A) Lie on your back on a mat or a work surface. You'll most likely want to support yourself using a desk chair or bench.  (B) Here are some dynamic stretches to get you started : [substeps] Sit on the floor with your head low and your feet shoulder width apart. Bring your hands near your mouth (palms facing down), locking your fingers together and pressing them into your middle molars.  (C) Stand tall with your feet shoulder width apart, and rest your hands on your quads or extend your arms in front of you. Keep your back straight, bend your knees, and lower your hips as if you were sitting in a chair.  (D) As you stretch, try to roll your knee a few times in a circular motion. This will help to strengthen your hip and thigh muscles, which your knee should move in a specific way.
answer: (C) Stand tall with your feet shoulder width apart, and rest your hands on your quads or extend your arms in front of you. Keep your back straight, bend your knees, and lower your hips as if you were sitting in a chair.

[header] How to buy a ceiling fan [title] Measure the length and width of the room. [step] Before you go ahead and buy your new ceiling fan, there are a number of factors and variables that you need to take into account. The first of these is the size of the room you are buying the fan for.
(A) The size of the room a fan fits comfortably in. [substeps] Take the height and width of the room into account.  (B) [substeps] Ideally, the ceiling fan should be at least as wide as the room's ceiling area. However, in large rooms, it can be as wide as the space between the ceiling opening and the wall of the room.  (C) Calculate these by weighing the ceiling fan at a traditional estimate of its height and width. [substeps] Btus is the height of the ceiling fan's widest edge (known as the flat edge).  (D) To make sure that the fan works effectively you need to ensure that it is big enough for the space. [substeps] Get hold of a tape measure and determine the length and width of the room.

question:
[header] How to note enclosures in a letter [title] Close your letter. [step] The enclosure notation occurs after the closing and signature of your letter. Use a complimentary closing such as " sincerely " or " yours truly, " leave space for your signature, and then type your name.
(A) [substeps] If you forget to write your thank you, skip right to the next step and type in " thanks. " this will either save the letter or find a pen and ink, which may not be necessary.  (B) Skip an important line in the salutation after each letter. [title] Write the paragraph right after the closing.  (C) [substeps] Generally, your closing might be more familiar if you are on more personal terms with the recipient. For example, if you are writing to your best friend's father, you might use a more informal closing such as " kind regards " or " best wishes.  (D) Add a signature block at the end, then close the letter inside. [substeps] Case for enclosures: i, john feeders, and tom jones, washington, illinois, may publish proposals for a cloche excavation project.
answer: (C) [substeps] Generally, your closing might be more familiar if you are on more personal terms with the recipient. For example, if you are writing to your best friend's father, you might use a more informal closing such as " kind regards " or " best wishes.

question:
[header] How to eat indian food with your hands [title] Wash your hands before you eat. [step] You want to ensure your hands are completely clean before you sit down for your meal. [title] Eat with your right hand only.
(A) [step] You'll want to pick that hand up and place it on your lap, right before you eat. The other hand remains upright; however, you can take it to your left side, if that's the way you like it.  (B) [step] Eating with your left hand helps to maintain control of the nose, mouth, and throat. However, this doesn't mean you should eat with the left hand.  (C) [step] In indian culture, the left hand is commonly viewed as dirty and unsanitary, and therefore rude to eat with. Avoid serving, eating, or touching any of the food with your left hand.  (D) [step] This will help minimize your risk of choking on food that is outside of your hand reach. Additionally, keeping the palm of your right hand in your left hand will help prevent spreading food from your hand and throat to other parts of your body.
answer: (C) [step] In indian culture, the left hand is commonly viewed as dirty and unsanitary, and therefore rude to eat with. Avoid serving, eating, or touching any of the food with your left hand.

question:
[header] How to do a back limber [title] Perfect your handstand. [step] The handstand is a basic gymnastic move that you'll need to be fully capable of doing in order to execute a back limber. If your handstand is a little rusty, practice until you can do it easily without the help of a spotter.
(A) [substeps] Sit on the floor with your back straight, with your feet shoulder width apart. Be sure that you're still upright, with your back straight.  (B) [substeps] Practice using good form-keep your shoulders in and your legs together. Be confident that you can hold your handstand for several seconds.  (C) [substeps] You'll need to be properly trained in yoga to perform the handstand. Vascular strength and stability will help strengthen your back and prepare you for the sit-ups and pulling-ups that will commonly be required with core and upper body strength training.  (D) Just because you're good with your hands doesn't mean you can't master the handstand. There's no need to beat anybody just to learn the handstand.
answer: (B) [substeps] Practice using good form-keep your shoulders in and your legs together. Be confident that you can hold your handstand for several seconds.

question:
[header] How to cook moose meat [title] Cut 2 ½ pounds (1134 g) of moose meat into 1 in (2.5 cm) cubes. [step] Unwrap your packaged raw moose meat and place it on a clean surface. Use a sharp knife to cube the moose meat into bite-sized pieces.
(A) Repeat this step with another piece of moose meat about 1 in (2.5 cm) bigger. [substeps] If you want the moose meat thicker, use 6-7 pieces of turkey meat.  (B) [title] Melt shortening in a large stew pot over medium-high heat. [step] Add 2 us tbsp (30 ml) of shortening to a large stew pot pan.  (C) [substeps] You can substitute sporello meat or specialty moose meat instead of raw moose meat if you prefer. [title] Wash your moose meat with warm water.  (D) While it's edible, fresh moose meat usually contains less fat than frozen options. [substeps] Don't overrefrigerate a frozen moose meat, as it may retain its freshness.
answer: (B) [title] Melt shortening in a large stew pot over medium-high heat. [step] Add 2 us tbsp (30 ml) of shortening to a large stew pot pan.

[header] How to develop interest in research [title] Relate the topic to yourself. [step] The easiest way to develop an interest in research is to look into something that affects you personally, or that will affect your life or one of your interests in some important way. If you're interested in the topic you're researching, you'll enjoy reading and learning about it.
(A) Sit in front of a mirror and try to draw pictures in your head of how you respond to situations and settings. If you're doing research to gain experience, this may seem like a guidebook.  (B) The other option is to reflect on why you're interested in it so you can start to link to it in the future. [substeps] Don't think of it as being something you enjoy.  (C) [substeps] If you're writing a research paper for a class, your professor may have given you a broad topic. Your project can be any smaller thing within that topic, so try to find something that affects you personally.  (D) [substeps] For example, you may feel an interest in cancer. You may start a work study of the condition, but reading about the disease right from the start of the study will keep you involved in the research.

[header] How to buy clothes while traveling [title] Pack travel soap. [step] This will reduce the amount of money you have to spend on clothes while traveling. Many travel stores sell small laundry soap sheets that dissolve and lather when placed in a sink full of water, so you don't have to carry extra liquids.
(A) [title] Pack 2 to 3 changes of clothing to get you through the first few days. [step] Even if you plan on checking luggage, it is a good idea to carry a few clothing changes with you, in case your luggage is lost or delayed.  (B) To save money in the first place, include traveler's-sized bottles of liquid laundry detergent in your travel supply pack. [substeps] If you're traveling with friends or family, you might want to ask a friend or a family member to help you transport their clothes for you.  (C) If you use an extra dip, like waeau de toilette or vaseline, there's no need to put additional items in your travel bag. [substeps] One diet that uses waeau de toilette and vaseline is ginkgo biloba.  (D) Petroleum-based laundry detergent can be purchasing through websites like eyeglass beauty or secondhand. Some stores also sell specialty toiletries and soap options, such as hengsome that you can have in the car, which you can use while on vacation.

(A) [title] Determine how far your awning should reach. [substeps] Covering a patio will require a larger awning than an awning covering a window or doorway.  (B) Limit the sun if you want a transparent screen. Check out the distance from the house's roof to the location of the awning.  (C) Determine the dimensions of the frames on your awning. You will want your awning to cover the distance of your balcony door and the perimeter of your home if you plan to use it outdoors.  (D) [title] Measure the length of the awning. [substeps] If the awning is long enough, or if there are any gaps near the awning, divide the awning's length by six to determine.

question:
[header] How to be environmentally friendly [title] Conserve water at home. [step] It takes a lot of energy to bring water from rivers, groundwater, or whatever your local water source is into your home. Water must be pumped to a treatment plant, filtered and treated with chemicals to clean it, then pumped into your neighborhood so you can use it home.
(A) It may sound intimidating but it's actually very important that you have gallons of water every day. Thinking of this as a necessity, and feeling the water you use everyday increases your infrastructure and may benefit your children as well.  (B) Saving as much water as possible reduces the stress this whole process puts on natural bodies of water in the environment. Here are a few ways to conserve water : [substeps] Use the low-water dishwashing method.  (C) You can use water from rainwater, recycled water from bottled water, or any other source that you can change locally. [substeps] Buying water from a local water source may mean running your tap over, rinsing your filters, and removing filter sediment and dead, dried out water.  (D) Water treatment chemicals come in six basic categories. The first one is chlorine, which is concentrated in the water and removes slowly.
answer: (B) Saving as much water as possible reduces the stress this whole process puts on natural bodies of water in the environment. Here are a few ways to conserve water : [substeps] Use the low-water dishwashing method.

(B) Even if it's just a casual, part-time job, dressing up will help you make an impression. [substeps] Choose a nice blouse and dress pants or a skirt. question:
[header] How to send luggage to another state [title] Weigh and measure your bag. [step] Use a bathroom scale and a tape measure to take the weight and dimensions of each bag you're sending. Knowing the size and weight of your bag before you visit the ups or fedex store will speed up the process and let you know how much to expect to pay.
(A) [substeps] The airline's baggage section at ups will usually have each bag's weight and measurements ready. [title] Fill the bag with preferred currencies.  (B) [substeps] The heavier and bigger the bag, the more expensive it will generally be to ship. If you don't have a scale or a measuring tape at home, these services can be performed for you at a ups or fedex store.  (C) [substeps] Tape measure costs about 2 to 6 dollars. The larger the bag the better; six to eight bags per day can cost over $500.  (D) [substeps] Make sure to use the biggest scale in the dye store. Typically, the bigger the bag, the harder it will be to trick the ups into guessing at your bag price.
answer: (B) [substeps] The heavier and bigger the bag, the more expensive it will generally be to ship. If you don't have a scale or a measuring tape at home, these services can be performed for you at a ups or fedex store.

question:
[header] How to have sex during pregnancy [title] Use lots of lubrication. [step] Try personal lubricants for more comfortable sex during pregnancy. Hormonal shifts while pregnant may make it easier or more difficult to produce your own natural lubrication.
(A) Since pregnancy stimulates sperm, it's important to provide adequate lubrication during pregnancy. [substeps] Wet the penis, scrotum, and rectum, using lubricating oils such as propyl (20 ml) or cystine (3 %).  (B) Consider touching your partner in bed or with your arms around him or her during intimacy. Try using distilled or booster lubricants for the rest of your activities.  (C) To avoid any vaginal discomfort during sex, use as much lube as you need to feel comfortable. [substeps] You can buy personal lubricants online or at your local drug store.  (D) Try choosing one or two types of vaginal lubricants that are oil-free and sealable, since these are less likely to slough off when pregnant. [substeps] Synthetic or silicone lubricants are some of the more lubrictive, but they won't work for pregnant women.
answer: (C) To avoid any vaginal discomfort during sex, use as much lube as you need to feel comfortable. [substeps] You can buy personal lubricants online or at your local drug store.

question:
[header] How to use conversations in google inbox [title] Tap a conversation to open it. [step] A conversation, or " thread ", is a collection of emails based on the subject. When you send out an email, any replies you get from any of the recipients are grouped together in one conversation.
(A) Each email, however, is labelled with a secret word or phrase which indicates that it is private. Other messages that contain secret words used by all the recipients are grouped into one conversation.  (B) Likewise, if you reply to an email that you receive, a new conversation is created. [substeps] A conversation is like a forum thread, where all relevant replies are kept in one location.  (C) The messages will be shifted around among recipients. If your conversation is sent this way, you'll get different response responses.  (D) The messages will now appear on your timeline and your inbox. [title] Tap the conversation icon to launch it.
answer: (B) Likewise, if you reply to an email that you receive, a new conversation is created. [substeps] A conversation is like a forum thread, where all relevant replies are kept in one location.

question:
[header] How to do your hair for school [title] Make a trendy ponytail. [step] With one hand, brush or pull your hair back, trying to avoid fly-away strands. With the other hand, grab a hair tie and slip the ponytail through the elastic.
(A) Twist the elastic, forming a figure-8 and pull the hair through again. Keep twisting the elastic and pulling your hair through until the elastic is tight.  (B) If your hair is thicker, use the hair tie to pull your ponytail through the elastic. [substeps] Smooth out any flyaways with a brush.  (C) Then, use your fingers to secure the hair on the side of your head. [substeps] [substeps] Try to have part of the ponytail hanging slightly out of the front of your hair, so that the side of your head still gets more hair on the front.  (D) The top of the ponytail should be about halfway up your head, giving you room to pull the elastic all the way through. At some point, the ponytail will come loose and get tangled.
answer: (A) Twist the elastic, forming a figure-8 and pull the hair through again. Keep twisting the elastic and pulling your hair through until the elastic is tight.

question:
[header] How to prevent benzodiazepine overdose [title] Stick to the recommended dose. [step] The easiest way to avoid overdose is to follow the medication dosage provided by your doctor. Generally, you will start on a smaller dose, but your doctor may gradually move you up over time if you begin to develop a tolerance to the medication.
(A) There are many different medication options on the market, including : [substeps] Benzodiazepine. This medication is regulated by the government and is available to everyone over the age of 55.  (B) Either way, sticking to the prescribed dosage should help you avoid an overdose. [substeps] If you're unclear about how often you should take your medication, talk to your doctor or pharmacist.  (C) [substeps] For example, changing your dosage twice a day may help, but other restrictions should be given. The key is not to take too much at once.  (D) [substeps] At first, the entire dose should be given slowly. However, over time, a dosage greater than 20% of your total dose may be increased.
answer: (B) Either way, sticking to the prescribed dosage should help you avoid an overdose. [substeps] If you're unclear about how often you should take your medication, talk to your doctor or pharmacist.

[header] How to get to get fda approval to market a medical device [title] Determine the classification of your medical device. [substeps] There are 3 classes: class i, ii, and iii. They are classified based on risk, with class i being the lowest and class iii being the greatest.
(A) You will find out what pre-marketing submission is required by fda based on classification of your device. General controls are assigned as class i either with or without exemptions.  (B) Ii is considered the biggest (least common of the 3 classes), and iv is considered the weakest (most common in aids which comes under administration of the drug). Iii is considered the smallest (by clinical examination only), though it occurs to larger numbers of people.  (C) Class ii is designed for normal people under 0.5. Iii is designed for people under 2 years of age.  (D) Classes ii and iii are on the higher ranks because you can offer better equipment, more medical devices, and anyone you wish. Class iii is faster and easier to use than ii or iii but is also more expensive.
answer: (A) You will find out what pre-marketing submission is required by fda based on classification of your device. General controls are assigned as class i either with or without exemptions.

[header] How to find a mesothelioma attorney [title] Determine the jurisdiction. [step] Before you get started on the search for an attorney, you must make sure you are clear about the appropriate county, or parish, and state in which to bring your claim. A lawsuit should be brought in the county, or parish, and state in which the person you are suing, called the defendant, lives or operates a business.
(A) You should obtain a general business office in each county where the person you are suing lives or operates a business. Consult your general business offices for use by you.  (B) [substeps] Do not limit your search to your local community. Your case may be filed in a different state to where you live.  (C) Filing in the county may be a more efficient means to initiate a lawsuit. [substeps] In a civil case, you can file a complaint in the local court.  (D) [substeps] If you follow the guidebook below for where to find an attorney, find one. It's a matter of personal preference.
answer: (B) [substeps] Do not limit your search to your local community. Your case may be filed in a different state to where you live.

question:
[header] How to care for a child with metabolic disorders [title] Contact a medical professional to run the appropriate tests and to arrive at an official diagnosis. [step] If your child has not been officially diagnosed by a physician and you suspect she has a metabolic disorder, you should contact a doctor immediately. The physician will run blood work and other tests in order to gather important data needed for a diagnosis.
(A) [title] Use imaging to determine your child's metabolic disorder. [step] Imaging to determine metabolic disorders includes : [substeps] Skin scans.  (B) Your physician might even refer you to a specialist or others who might be important members of your medical support team. [substeps] Be prepared for a wide array of tests, including blood work.  (C) She may order a neurological exam, check for hemoglobin levels, and bone marrow tests. She may also suggest that her child see a neurological specialist if her metabolic disorder is not already diagnosed.  (D) [substeps] However, most primary care doctors will expect the symptoms to go away on their own. It's never a good idea to spread this disease to a child that is diagnosed alone.

This allows a married couple who are unsure whether or not they want a divorce to live separately and determine if the marriage can be fixed.
(A) During a legal separation, the following rights or responsibilities may be accorded to the parties : [substeps] A court may establish that one spouse should receive alimony. A court can approve a child custody arrangement and child support.  (B) Such decisions allow a judge to make specific decisions about where a marriage should be dissolved. Think about trying a legal separation, if it takes a lot of work and funds to keep the marriage going through divorce. [header] How to make golden snitch hanging decorations [title] Paint the top half of the polystyrene balls gold using the small brush. [step] Two thin coats are better than one thick, so it won't look great at first, don't panic! While they dry, find a circular hollow to rest them so they don't roll about. Leave to dry completely.
(A) [title] Paint two, brick-sized gold balls in the gold paint. [step] A light brown color may work best.  (B) [title] Paint gold with a thin-formed circle at the top. [step] Add two balls of the same color at the top to make the holes for the bottles.  (C) [title] Using a styptic lip pencil, create several tiny dots with the pencil brush with dark brown and white over the center of the balls, each side scuffed to resemble circles. [step] Their shape is entirely up to your imagination.  (D) [title] When dry, paint the bottom halves and leave to dry again. [title] Repeat with a second coat so no white is showing through.
answer: (D) [title] When dry, paint the bottom halves and leave to dry again. [title] Repeat with a second coat so no white is showing through.

question:
[header] How to make laundry smell good [title] Spray your dirty laundry with essential oil. [step] Add a few drops of your favorite scented oil to a small spray bottle. Fill it with water and give it a few shakes.
(A) Mist your dirty clothes with it before adding them to the washer. [title] Use scented detergents or laundry soap.  (B) The amount depends upon the material of your fabric, but some essential oils will produce an earthy aroma while others require producing a more potent smell. This willn't take long to achieve.  (C) Close the bottle and shake it to see how much of it you need to add to the cleaning solution. [substeps] Using essential oils will make your laundry smell better and make the cleaning solution smell better, since the scent will already have already been absorbed through the fabric.  (D) Then, add a few drops of your regular essential oil (peppermint essential oil) to the bottle of laundry detergent. Mix the solution, in which you can add a drop of bubble or raspberry essential oil (aloe vera or rose) or let it sit for a few minutes.
answer: (A) Mist your dirty clothes with it before adding them to the washer. [title] Use scented detergents or laundry soap.

question:
[header] How to not miss someone [title] Keep a count-down. [step] Mark off each day on the calendar and congratulate yourself on getting through the day. Focus on living one day at a time.
(A) Resist the urge to keep busy and do something else instead. Carve out 3 months to spend alone with someone you love.  (B) A loved one's absence shifts life into a different gear. For the person left to manage life when the other person is gone, it is essential to focus on successfully getting through each day! [title] Focus on other relationships.  (C) Do your best not to let fear or disappointment get the best of you; be happy! Even if you are worried, remind yourself that this moment belongs to you. Here are some examples : [substeps] When you go home, appreciate the length of the day with a smile.  (D) If it's just one day, you're more likely to miss it. [substeps] It may help to log your progress daily.
answer: (B) A loved one's absence shifts life into a different gear. For the person left to manage life when the other person is gone, it is essential to focus on successfully getting through each day! [title] Focus on other relationships.

question:
[header] How to compare loan rates [title] Consider fixed versus adjustable interest rates for a loan. [step] Financial experts report that many lenders in recent years have pushed adjustable interest rates without really telling people what they are. A borrower should know whether their loan includes a fixed or adjustable interest rate.
(A) A fixed interest rate does not necessarily have to be fixed, but it should not be removed from the loan to lower the interest rate, as it depreciate in interest. [substeps] You should try to buy a fixed loan for a lower rate, such as 30% or 25 %.  (B) [substeps] Fixed interest rates are recorded by a number of different sources. The pinwheel accuracy of their accounts is another important factor.  (C) A fixed interest rate rate is an amount you can borrow and then purchase with interest, typically for less than $100. [substeps] A fixed interest rate is an amount you can buy and then purchase at the same time without needing to pay interest.  (D) [substeps] Understand the benefits and disadvantages of adjustable interest rates. With a fixed interest rate, the borrower knows what interest they will pay on a loan over the entire term of the loan.
answer: (D) [substeps] Understand the benefits and disadvantages of adjustable interest rates. With a fixed interest rate, the borrower knows what interest they will pay on a loan over the entire term of the loan.

[header] How to do quick chores your parents will appreciate [title] Clean the tv screens. [step] These tend to get dirty and dusty, and you don't really notice it until you are trying to watch a show. It's a lot easier to see the dusty and dirty spots if you turn the tv off first.
(A) Lift all the screens out of their frames, place the tv in a secure position, and let them air dry. [substeps] Give the most effective chores to your parents by making sure that the screens have good ventilation.  (B) You might want to wipe off remotes and game controllers, while you are at it. [substeps] Remove the dust with a used dryer sheet-it will attract dust and keep it from returning to the areas you've already cleaned.  (C) You probably don't even notice most messy screens until you try to do the dishes in the back of the room. It's also a good idea to clean at least a couple of the screen trays to get out the rubbish.  (D) In fact, if you turn the tv back on you're giving your parents time to clean up too. [substeps] Clean screens with the same cleaners you're already taking.
answer: (B) You might want to wipe off remotes and game controllers, while you are at it. [substeps] Remove the dust with a used dryer sheet-it will attract dust and keep it from returning to the areas you've already cleaned.

(A) [step] Navigating to where the internet is, establishing a encrypted connection, and accessing an advanced web browser, will sometimes seem very daunting. (B) [step] Multiple connections are best; otherwise your website will be down whenever there's an interruption in your internet connection. [title] Find a stable system for your computer.  (C) [step] Vls5 is another option that might save you some trouble choosing one if you're looking for a good host. [substeps] A quick internet browser will usually work fine in a remote server.  (D) [step] Easy access to your internet server is via an ethernet cable. Internet hotspots always have a built in router to use, and you may need to turn your router on or off, depending on the router.

[header] How to quickly do a reading log [title] Commit to prep works. [step] You may not want to devote much time to prepping for your reading log. However, a little prep work will help you stick with the log and finish it efficiently.
(A) You can commit to prep work for a while. Place each page in its own place on your log so that you have where it should go.  (B) [substeps] Make sure to spend time planning what you are going to say. Have a general idea of what your sentence is going to say, and then make an outline.  (C) Some of your prep work might include : [substeps] Composing the text into a spoken script. This process will usually involve creating a cover letter.  (D) [title] Find a book that you enjoy reading. [step] If your teacher allows you to select which book you want to read, you can find one that you enjoy reading.
answer: (D) [title] Find a book that you enjoy reading. [step] If your teacher allows you to select which book you want to read, you can find one that you enjoy reading.

question:
[header] How to make a healthy cream cheese and turkey snack [title] Thoroughly wash your hands since you'll be handling food. [step] You may also use latex, vinyl, or plastic gloves if you wish. [title] Prepare your workspace.
(A) [step] Make sure that you have an air well ventilated space to work near the food you'll be using. [title] Quickly erase any food residue from your food.  (B) [step] Make sure that there is not a mess, mix everything together, and set it aside for later use. Place the container with the packaged ingredients onto your work surface.  (C) [step] Clean and wipe a counter-top dry, use a clean cutting board, or spread a small area of aluminum foil to work on. [title] Lay one slice of the two turkey slices on the working surface.  (D) [step] Lay out a table or work surface. It should be in a warm, well ventilated place.
answer: (C) [step] Clean and wipe a counter-top dry, use a clean cutting board, or spread a small area of aluminum foil to work on. [title] Lay one slice of the two turkey slices on the working surface.

def parse_music(music_string: str) -> List[int]:

    not last.

    Here is a legend:
    'o' - whole note, lasts four beats
    'o|' - half note, lasts two beats
    '.|' - quater note, lasts one beat

    >>> parse_music('o o| .| o| o| .| .| .| .| o o')
    [4, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1, 1, 1, 1, 4, 4]
    """

question:
Complete the following Python function:

def tri(n):
    """Everyone knows Fibonacci sequence, it was studied deeply by mathematicians in 
    the last couple centuries. However, what people don't know is Tribonacci sequence.
    Tribonacci sequence is defined by the recurrence:
    tri(1) = 3
    tri(n) = 1 + n / 2, if n is even.
    tri(n) =  tri(n - 1) + tri(n - 2) + tri(n + 1), if n is odd.
    For example:
    tri(2) = 1 + (2 / 2) = 2
    tri(4) = 3
    tri(3) = tri(2) + tri(1) + tri(4)
           = 2 + 3 + 3 = 8 
    You are given a non-negative integer number n, you have to a return a list of the 
    first n + 1 numbers of the Tribonacci sequence.
    Examples:
    tri(3) = [1, 3, 2, 8]
    """

    assert list_sort(["aa", "a", "aaa"]) => ["aa"]

    """

question:
Complete the following Python function:

def will_it_fly(q,w):
    '''
    Write a function that returns True if the object q will fly, and False otherwise.
    The object q will fly if it's balanced (it is a palindromic list) and the sum of its elements is less than or equal the maximum possible weight w.

    Example:
    will_it_fly([1, 2], 5) ➞ False 
    # 1+2 is less than the maximum possible weight, but it's unbalanced.

    will_it_fly([3, 2, 3], 1) ➞ False
    # it's balanced, but 3+2+3 is more than the maximum possible weight.

    will_it_fly([3, 2, 3], 9) ➞ True
    # 3+2+3 is less than the maximum possible weight, and it's balanced.

    will_it_fly([3], 5) ➞ True
    # 3 is less than the maximum possible weight, and it's balanced.
    '''

    """

question:
Complete the following Python function:

def encode_cyclic(s: str):
    """
    returns encoded string by cycling groups of three characters.
    """
    # split string to groups. Each of length 3.
    groups = [s[(3 * i):min((3 * i + 3), len(s))] for i in range((len(s) + 2) // 3)]
    # cycle elements in each group. Unless group has fewer elements than 3.
    groups = [(group[1:] + group[0]) if len(group) == 3 else group for group in groups]
    return "".join(groups)


def decode_cyclic(s: str):
    """
    takes as input string encoded with encode_cyclic function. Returns decoded string.
    """

def is_palindrome(string: str) -> bool:
    """ Test if given string is a palindrome """
    return string == string[::-1]


def make_palindrome(string: str) -> str:
    """ Find the shortest palindrome that begins with a supplied string.
    Algorithm idea is simple:
    - Find the longest postfix of supplied string that is a palindrome.
    - Append to the end of the string reverse of a string prefix that comes before the palindromic suffix.
    >>> make_palindrome('')
    ''
    >>> make_palindrome('cat')
    'catac'
    >>> make_palindrome('cata')
    'catac'
    """

    move_one_ball([3, 5, 4, 1, 2])==>False

                array by performing any number of right shift operations.
                
    """

question:
Complete the following Python function:

def minPath(grid, k):
    """
    Given a grid with N rows and N columns (N >= 2) and a positive integer k, 
    each cell of the grid contains a value. Every integer in the range [1, N * N]
    inclusive appears exactly once on the cells of the grid.

    You have to find the minimum path of length k in the grid. You can start
    from any cell, and in each step you can move to any of the neighbor cells,
    in other words, you can go to cells which share an edge with you current
    cell.
    Please note that a path of length k means visiting exactly k cells (not
    necessarily distinct).
    You CANNOT go off the grid.
    A path A (of length k) is considered less than a path B (of length k) if
    after making the ordered lists of the values on the cells that A and B go
    through (let's call them lst_A and lst_B), lst_A is lexicographically less
    than lst_B, in other words, there exist an integer index i (1 <= i <= k)
    such that lst_A[i] < lst_B[i] and for any j (1 <= j < i) we have
    lst_A[j] = lst_B[j].
    It is guaranteed that the answer is unique.
    Return an ordered list of the values on the cells that the minimum path go through.

    Examples:

        Input: grid = [ [1,2,3], [4,5,6], [7,8,9]], k = 3
        Output: [1, 2, 1]

        Input: grid = [ [5,9,3], [4,1,6], [7,8,2]], k = 1
        Output: [1]
    """

formats.A:

def by_length(arr):
    """
    Given an array of integers, sort the integers that are between 1 and 9 inclusive,
    reverse the resulting array, and then replace each digit by its corresponding name from
    "One", "Two", "Three", "Four", "Five", "Six", "Seven", "Eight", "Nine".

    For example:
      arr = [2, 1, 1, 4, 5, 8, 2, 3]   
            -> sort arr -> [1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8] 
            -> reverse arr -> [8, 5, 4, 3, 2, 2, 1, 1]
      return ["Eight", "Five", "Four", "Three", "Two", "Two", "One", "One"]
    
      If the array is empty, return an empty array:
      arr = []
      return []
    
      If the array has any strange number ignore it:
      arr = [1, -1 , 55] 
            -> sort arr -> [-1, 1, 55]
            -> reverse arr -> [55, 1, -1]
      return = ['One']
    """

    expression and return the evaluation of this expression.

    operator['+', '*', '-']
    array = [2, 3, 4, 5]
    result = 2 + 3 * 4 - 5
    => result = 9

    Note:
        The length of operator list is equal to the length of operand list minus one.
        Operand is a list of of non-negative integers.
        Operator list has at least one operator, and operand list has at least two operands.

    """

def car_race_collision(n: int):
    """
    Imagine a road that's a perfectly straight infinitely long line.
    n cars are driving left to right;  simultaneously, a different set of n cars
    are driving right to left.   The two sets of cars start out being very far from

    when a car that's moving left to right hits a car that's moving right to left.
    However, the cars are infinitely sturdy and strong; as a result, they continue moving
    in their trajectory as if they did not collide.

    This function outputs the number of such collisions.
    """

import math


def poly(xs: list, x: float):
    """
    Evaluates polynomial with coefficients xs at point x.
    return xs[0] + xs[1] * x + xs[1] * x^2 + .... xs[n] * x^n
    """
    return sum([coeff * math.pow(x, i) for i, coeff in enumerate(xs)])


def find_zero(xs: list):
    """ xs are coefficients of a polynomial.
    find_zero find x such that poly(x) = 0.
    find_zero returns only only zero point, even if there are many.
    Moreover, find_zero only takes list xs having even number of coefficients
    and largest non zero coefficient as it guarantees
    a solution.
    >>> round(find_zero([1, 2]), 2) # f(x) = 1 + 2x
    -0.5
    >>> round(find_zero([-6, 11, -6, 1]), 2) # (x - 1) * (x - 2) * (x - 3) = -6 + 11x - 6x^2 + x^3
    1.0
    """

def Strongest_Extension(class_name, extensions):
    """You will be given the name of a class (a string) and a list of extensions.
    The extensions are to be used to load additional classes to the class. The
    strength of the extension is as follows: Let CAP be the number of the uppercase
    letters in the extension's name, and let SM be the number of lowercase letters 
    in the extension's name, the strength is given by the fraction CAP - SM. 
    You should find the strongest extension and return a string in this 
    format: ClassName.StrongestExtensionName.
    If there are two or more extensions with the same strength, you should
    choose the one that comes first in the list.
    For example, if you are given "Slices" as the class and a list of the
    extensions: ['SErviNGSliCes', 'Cheese', 'StuFfed'] then you should
    return 'Slices.SErviNGSliCes' since 'SErviNGSliCes' is the strongest extension 
    (its strength is -1).
    Example:
    for Strongest_Extension('my_class', ['AA', 'Be', 'CC']) == 'my_class.AA'
    """

Complete the following Python function:

def triangle_area(a, b, c):
    '''
    Given the lengths of the three sides of a triangle. Return the area of
    the triangle rounded to 2 decimal points if the three sides form a valid triangle. 
    Otherwise return -1
    Three sides make a valid triangle when the sum of any two sides is greater 
    than the third side.
    Example:
    triangle_area(3, 4, 5) == 6.00
    triangle_area(1, 2, 10) == -1
    '''

question:
As reported on CNN, in a May 1999 national poll 43% of high school students expressed fear about going to school. Which of the following best describes what is meant by the poll having a margin of error of 5%?
(A) There is a 0.05 probability that the 43% result is in error.  (B) The pollsters are 95% confident that the true proportion is between 38% and 48%.  (C) Five percent of the students refused to participate in the poll.  (D) The poll's results could be 5% higher or lower due to random chance.  (E) Five percent of the students surveyed did not understand the question.  (F) There is a 5% chance that the results of the poll are completely inaccurate.  (G) Only 5% of the students surveyed gave valid responses.  (H) It is likely that the true proportion of high school students afraid to go to school is between 38% and 48%.  (I) The poll's results are 5% away from the true proportion.  (J) Between 38% and 48% of those surveyed expressed fear about going to school.
answer: (B) The pollsters are 95% confident that the true proportion is between 38% and 48%.

question:
A farmer who raises hens for egg production wants his eggs to have a mean mass of 56 grams. He is considering the purchase of a different type of hen, so he took a random sample of 18 eggs laid by this type of hen. The distribution of the masses is symmetric and mound-shaped with a mean of 54.1 grams and no outliers. The farmer conducted a t-test to see if there is evidence that the eggs from these hens have a mean mass that is different from 56 g and got a test statistic of t= -1.973. If he uses a 5% significance level, which is the correct conclusion and reason?
(A) Because tis more extreme than ±2.00, he should reject the null hypothesis. He has convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (B) Because tis more extreme than ±1.96, he should reject the null hypothesis. He has convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (C) Because tis less extreme than the critical value of t for 17 degrees of freedom, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (D) Because tis less extreme than the critical value of t for 20 degrees of freedom, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (E) Because tis more extreme than the critical value of t for 16 degrees of freedom, he should reject the null hypothesis. He has convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (F) Because tis less extreme than the critical value of t for 16 degrees of freedom, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (G) Because tis more extreme than the critical value of t for 20 degrees of freedom, he should reject the null hypothesis. He has convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (H) Because tis more extreme than the critical value of t for 18 degrees of freedom, he should reject the null hypothesis. He has convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (I) Because tis less extreme than ±2.00, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.  (J) Because tis less extreme than the critical value of t for 18 degrees of freedom, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.
answer: (C) Because tis less extreme than the critical value of t for 17 degrees of freedom, he should not reject the null hypothesis. He does not have convincing evidence at the 5% significance level that the mean mass of eggs from these hens is different from 56 grams.

question:
The director of a local food bank asks for data on all donations given during the month of November. Of the 100 checks received, the average donation is $155 with a standard deviation of $32. Which of the following is the most appropriate statement?
(A) We are 95% confident that the average donation in November is between about $149 and $161.  (B) The average donation this November is $32.  (C) We are 95% confident that the average donation in November is between about $123 and $187.  (D) This November, the average donation is $155.  (E) This November, the standard deviation of donations is $155.  (F) 75% of all donations this November are more than $155.  (G) We are 95% confident that the average donation in November is between about $91 and $219.  (H) 50% of all donations this November are less than $155.  (I) 50% of all donations this November are more than $155.  (J) We are 99% confident that the average donation in November is between about $91 and $219.
answer: (A) We are 95% confident that the average donation in November is between about $149 and $161.

The bottom of the ladder rests on a ledge and leans across the moat in equilibrium against a frictionless, vertical castle wall. The ladder makes an angle of with the horizontal. Lancelot pauses onethird of the way up the ladder.  Find the magnitude of the contact force on the base of the ladder. (Unit: N)
(A) 870 N  (B) 1050 N  (C) 1100  (D) 980  (E) 1200 N  (F) 950  (G) 930 N  (H) 1250 N  (I) 1020  (J) 890 N

question:
A solid, metal object is isolated from other charges and has charge distributed on its surface. The charge distribution is not uniform. It may be correctly concluded that the
(A) the electric field inside the object, but very close to the surface, is equal to the surface charge density at any location divided by the permittivity of free space  (B) the electric field outside the object, but very close to the surface, is equal to the surface charge density at any location divided by the permittivity of free space  (C) the electric field inside the object is directly proportional to the distance away from the center of mass of the object  (D) electric field outside the object is zero  (E) the electric field outside the object is inversely proportional to the distance away from the center of mass of the object  (F) the electric field outside the object, but very close to the surface, is equal to the permittivity of free space divided by the surface charge density at any location  (G) the electric field outside the object, but very close to the surface, is inversely proportional to the surface charge density at any location divided by the permittivity of free space  (H) the electric field outside the object is equal to the electric field inside the object  (I) the electric field inside the object is zero  (J) the electric field outside the object is directly proportional to the distance away from the center of mass of the object
answer: (B) the electric field outside the object, but very close to the surface, is equal to the surface charge density at any location divided by the permittivity of free space
